title: Pittosporum engleri
taxonomy: Pittosporum engleri is classified within the family Pittosporaceae. This family consists of approximately 200 species of flowering plants. Pittosporaceae belongs to the order Apiales, which is part of the larger clade of asterids. The family Pittosporaceae is native to the Southern Hemisphere, specifically in Australia, New Zealand, and parts of Asia and South America. The classification of Pittosporum engleri reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within this family.

intro: Pittosporum engleri, also known as Engler's Pittosporum, is a plant species belonging to the Pittosporaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it a potential remedy for various ailments such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Pittosporum engleri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
